page: Amazon Web Services m5d.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, supported by a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 376.29 GB/sec and uncached large block transfers at 48.61 GB/sec. Single-core performance falls into the weaker tier, as evidenced by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores. Cryptographic and extended instruction tests show average capabilities, while database operations, Redis SET operations at 6.88 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 6.93 GB/sec perform at a moderate level. Large language model inference speeds are weak to poor, particularly for prompt processing on medium-sized models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5d.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile by combining dedicated compute, DDR4 memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage without requiring separate storage attachment fees. This makes it suitable for workloads that benefit from low-latency local scratch space, such as database operations, caching layers, software compilation, and general-purpose web serving. It is less suitable for compute-intensive single-threaded tasks or large-scale AI inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 3.1 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside weaker single-core and LLM inference metrics. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a balance of compute, memory, and high-speed local storage. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching, software compilation, and general-purpose web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, delivering balanced performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU general-purpose instance with 32 GB RAM and a 300 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon compute, DDR4 memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.

32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
